title: Upgrade all 6 bootstrap templates — Shadow Mirroring hard rule protocol strengthening
description: Upgrade all 6 bootstrap templates to strengthen the fab_get_rules mandate from 'Before editing any file' to 'Before ANY code reading, architecture planning, or logic modification'. Add new hard rule: NEVER Reason about or modify code before obtaining local shadow context via MCP. Add explicit Shadow Mirroring concept explanation: business dirs have no rule files; rules centralized in .fabric/agents/. Bilingual format maintained: English hard rules (protected tokens not translated) + Chinese explanation block. The 6 files are: CLAUDE.md, cursor-fabric-bootstrap.mdc, GEMINI.md, codex-AGENTS-header.md, roo-fabric.md, windsurf-fabric.md. This addresses the 'perception-phase vacuum' (AI reading/planning code without fab_get_rules) that R3-CQ1 decision mandated fixing.

files.target: CORE RULES section — fab_get_rules MUST rule
files.change: Change 'Before editing any file, call the MCP tool fab_get_rules(path=<target file>)' to 'Before ANY code reading, architecture planning, or logic modification, call the MCP tool fab_get_rules(path=<target file>) to retrieve shadow constraints from .fabric/agents/'. Add new NEVER rule: 'NEVER: Reason about or modify code before obtaining local shadow context via MCP'. Add Shadow Mirroring concept in Chinese explanation: business dirs (src/, packages/) contain ZERO rule files; all rules centralized in .fabric/agents/ mirror tree.

Apply identical hard rule upgrade as CLAUDE.md: expand scope from 'editing' to 'code reading, architecture planning, or logic modification'; add NEVER rule; add Shadow Mirroring concept in Chinese explanation block.
Apply identical hard rule upgrade as CLAUDE.md.
Apply identical hard rule upgrade as CLAUDE.md.
Apply identical hard rule upgrade as CLAUDE.md.
Apply identical hard rule upgrade as CLAUDE.md.

implementation: Read all 6 bootstrap template files before editing to understand current structure and locate the fab_get_rules rule text
Identify exact rule text for fab_get_rules in each template (may differ slightly in phrasing)

Draft the upgraded rule text in English: 'MUST: Before ANY code reading, architecture planning, or logic modification, call the MCP tool fab_get_rules(path=<target file>) to retrieve shadow constraints from .fabric/agents/'
Draft the new NEVER rule: 'NEVER: Reason about or modify code before obtaining local shadow context via MCP'
Draft Shadow Mirroring concept for Chinese explanation block (2-3 sentences: business dirs have no rule files; all constraints in .fabric/agents/; fab_get_rules reads from this mirror tree)
Apply upgrades to CLAUDE.md first; review result before applying to remaining 5 templates
Apply same upgrade pattern to cursor-fabric-bootstrap.mdc, GEMINI.md, codex-AGENTS-header.md, roo-fabric.md, windsurf-fabric.md
Verify all 6 templates have the upgraded rule using grep across templates/bootstrap/

reference.pattern: CORE RULES section structure in templates/bootstrap/CLAUDE.md (bilingual English hard rules + Chinese explanation)
reference.examples: conclusions.json round3_delta.new_technical_solutions[1] specifies exact new rule text for bootstrap protocol upgrade

criteria: All 6 bootstrap template files contain the phrase 'Before ANY code reading, architecture planning, or logic modification' in the fab_get_rules MUST rule
All 6 bootstrap templates contain a NEVER rule referencing 'Reason about or modify code before obtaining local shadow context via MCP'
All 6 bootstrap templates mention .fabric/agents/ as the location of shadow constraints in the Chinese explanation section
English hard rule tokens (MUST, NEVER, fab_get_rules, .fabric/agents.meta.json, etc.) not translated in any template
grep 'Before editing any file' templates/bootstrap/* returns zero matches (old rule text replaced)

rationale.chosen_approach: Uniform upgrade across all 6 templates to ensure consistent protocol regardless of AI client used; keep bilingual format intact
alternatives_considered: Only upgrade CLAUDE.md (used by Claude Code) — incomplete since Cursor/Codex/Gemini clients would still have old rule
Generate a shared include file and @import in each template — adds dependency complexity; templates are intentionally self-contained
decision_factors: R3-CQ2: product requires MCP-capable clients; no fallback — all clients get the same strengthened protocol
R3-CQ1: perception-phase vacuum addressed by protocol not by bridge artifacts
Uniform upgrade prevents 'one client enforces, another does not' inconsistency
rationale.tradeoffs: 6 files to update manually; risk of inconsistent phrasing across templates mitigated by drafting standard text first and applying uniformly

manual_checks: grep -r 'Before ANY code reading' templates/bootstrap/ — must match all 6 files
grep -r 'Before editing any file' templates/bootstrap/ — must return zero matches
grep -r 'shadow context via MCP' templates/bootstrap/ — must match all 6 files
success_metrics: All 6 templates updated: grep confirms new rule text present in all 6 files
No regressions: protected tokens (MUST, NEVER, fab_get_rules) present and unaltered in all 6 files

risks.description: Some bootstrap templates may have different formatting that makes the edit location ambiguous
risks.mitigation: Read each template before editing to identify the exact fab_get_rules rule location
risks.fallback: If format significantly differs, adapt the upgrade to match each template's structure while preserving semantic equivalence
